steps.title: Identify the literary technique
steps.content: The phrase uses a simile—”like ancient trees in a petrified forest”—a comparison that creates a visual image. The surrounding text describes the columns' physical appearance: “forty feet tall, spindly but still miraculously upright.” The simile is serving a visual, descriptive purpose.

steps.title: Connect the technique to the answer
steps.content:
Similes create vivid images to help readers see something. This one compares the columns to ancient trees, illustrating what this part of the ruins looks like in terms of height, form, and condition—exactly what choice A describes.

The other choices misidentify the purpose:
  • B claims the phrase shows prior knowledge
  • C invents an expectation about forests
  • D treats it as historical information about when ruins formed
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
